skill.name: Azure.ResourceManager.ArizeAIObservabilityEval
skill.description: Azure Resource Manager SDK for Arize AI Observability and Evaluation in .NET applications
skill.summary: Manage Arize AI organizations on Azure via Azure Marketplace with this .NET SDK
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Documentation-only skill providing usage examples for Azure Resource Manager SDK. Static analysis scanned 0 files with 0 suspicious patterns detected. No executable code present - only markdown documentation with standard Azure authentication patterns using DefaultAzureCredential.

content.user_title: Manage Arize AI Resources on Azure with .NET
content.value_statement: Deploy and manage Arize AI ML observability organizations on Azure through the Azure Resource Manager SDK. Simplifies Azure Marketplace subscription workflows for machine learning teams.
actual_capabilities: Create Arize AI organizations in Azure Resource Manager
Retrieve and query existing Arize organization resources
Update organization tags and marketplace subscription details
List organizations by resource group or subscription
limitations: Requires valid Azure subscription and marketplace subscription to Arize AI
Does not interact directly with Arize AI API - only Azure ARM layer
Requires Azure SDK credentials configured in environment

use_cases.title: ML Platform Team Provisioning
use_cases.description: Automate Arize AI organization creation for new machine learning teams joining your Azure environment

use_cases.title: Resource Governance and Cleanup
use_cases.description: Audit and manage Arize AI resources across subscriptions with programmatic control

use_cases.title: Marketplace Subscription Management
use_cases.description: Streamline Arize AI marketplace subscription workflows for enterprise deployments

prompt_templates.title: Create Arize Organization
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a new Arize AI organization in Azure resource group 'ml-prod' located in East US with my marketplace subscription ID

prompt_templates.title: List Organizations
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me all Arize AI organizations in my Azure subscription and their current provisioning state

prompt_templates.title: Update Organization Tags
prompt_templates.prompt: Update the tags on my Arize organization 'my-arize-org' to add environment=production and team=data-science

prompt_templates.title: Delete Organization with Confirmation
prompt_templates.prompt: Guide me through safely deleting an Arize AI organization after verifying it is no longer in use

output_examples.input: Create Arize AI organization in East US
output_examples.output: Successfully created Arize AI organization 'my-arize-org' in resource group 'ml-prod'. Provisioning state: Succeeded. Resource ID contains subscription, resource group, and organization name.
Organization 'my-arize-org' exists. Properties: Location=EastUS, ProvisioningState=Succeeded, MarketplaceStatus=Subscribed, Tags={environment: production}

best_practices: Use DefaultAzureCredential for authentication to support multiple credential sources
Always use async methods with proper await patterns in .NET
Handle RequestFailedException with specific status codes for error scenarios
anti_patterns: Avoid hardcoded credentials - always use environment variables or managed identity
Do not ignore long-running operation results - wait for completion or poll status
Never skip error handling for 404 Not Found when checking resource existence

faq.question: What Azure permissions are required to use this SDK?
faq.answer: You need Microsoft.ArizeAi.ObservabilityEval/organizations write and read permissions at the resource group or subscription level.

faq.question: Does this SDK connect directly to Arize AI services?
faq.answer: No, this SDK only manages Azure Resource Manager resources. You need the Arize AI SDK separately to interact with Arize services.

faq.question: How do I authenticate with Azure using this skill?
faq.answer: Use DefaultAzureCredential which supports managed identity, Azure CLI, Visual Studio, and environment variable credentials automatically.

faq.question: What happens if my marketplace subscription is not active?
faq.answer: Organization creation will fail with an error indicating the marketplace subscription is not valid or active.

faq.question: Can I manage multiple Arize organizations with this SDK?
faq.answer: Yes, you can create, read, update, and delete multiple Arize AI organization resources across different resource groups and subscriptions.

faq.question: Is this SDK compatible with .NET Framework or only .NET Core?
faq.answer: The Azure SDK for .NET supports .NET Standard 2.0, which includes .NET Framework 4.6.1+, .NET Core 2.0+, and .NET 5+.
